summary: 12 countries. The eleventh straight night of US strikes on Iran leads coverage nearly everywhere, with Washington now putting the war's cost at $37.5 billion and Trump saying he has no interest in talks for now. Tariff fights are the second thread, from Canada and Mexico to Brazil and the African trade programme AGOA.

items.title: Middle East live: US launches 11th night of Iran strikes as diplomacy falters
US estimates cost of war in Iran at $37.5 billion as it renews strikes on Tehran
items.note: The figure comes from Hegseth's testimony to US senators and is up from about $29 billion in May.

items.title: U.S. attacks deepen hardship, not dissent, in Iran’s restive south

Trump says strikes on Iran will continue until he personally stops them
items.orig: Трамп заявил, что удары по Ирану продолжатся, пока он лично не остановит их

items.title: Trump says the US is destroying Iran at an unprecedented pace
items.orig: Трамп заявил, что США разрушают Иран беспрецедентными темпами
items.note: RT relays Trump's own claims about the pace of the campaign without independent confirmation.

items.note: Trump accused Ottawa of failing at basic forest management and said the cost of the smoke should be added to Canada's tariff bill.

items.title: Mexico, Canada ink strategic partnership pact and agree to seek 'fairer' trade deal with US
“The tariffs are working”: Trump credits his tariff policy for Toyota moving a plant from Mexico to the US
items.orig: “Los aranceles están surtiendo efecto”: Trump atribuye a política arancelaria el traslado de planta de Toyota de México a EU

items.title: Trump government prepares new tariffs for dozens of countries: “We expect news soon”, says Jamieson Greer
items.orig: Gobierno de Trump prepara nuevos aranceles para decenas de países; “Esperamos novedades pronto”, avanza Jamieson Greer
items.note: Mexican coverage expects a 10 percent levy tied to US claims about forced and child labour enforcement.

Why Kenya keeps surviving Trump’s bans
How Trump's tariffs threaten AGOA renewal and affect US-Africa trade
items.note: AGOA lapsed in September 2025. Kenyan and Lesotho garment exports are the most exposed if it is not restored.
